title: ECDH, X25519, and Authenticated Key Agreement · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed security review reconstructs ecdh, x25519, and authenticated key agreement from threat, algorithm, parameter, implementation, key-custody, failure, test-vector, operations, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for authenticated X25519 key-agreement transcript after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use supported ECDH curves through Go interfaces with private/public key separation, strict parsing, and shared-secret error handling.
Select X25519 or approved NIST curves from protocol, hardware, compliance, interoperability, and implementation constraints.
Feed the ECDH result and full context into a labeled KDF rather than using raw shared bytes directly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Crypto Go Ecdh Contract · changed case · Review Crypto Go Ecdh Contract
steps.instruction:
review case — A review cryptographic engineering team handles case 2915 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to authenticate and transcript-bind an ephemeral X25519 or approved ECDH exchange; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use supported ECDH curves through Go interfaces with private/public key separation, strict parsing, and shared-secret error handling.

Which action demonstrates Crypto Go Ecdh Contract without dropping earlier contracts?

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review cryptographic engineering team handles case 2201 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to authenticate and transcript-bind an ephemeral X25519 or approved ECDH exchange; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Rotate and erase ephemeral private keys, prevent reuse, bind roles, and state when compromise does or does not expose prior sessions.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Using elliptic curves automatically supplies forward secrecy.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rotate and erase ephemeral private keys, prevent reuse, bind roles, and state when compromise does or does not expose prior sessions. Artifact under review: authenticated X25519 key-agreement transcript.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, run one deterministic Crypto Go Forward Secrecy Ephemeral model, omit canonical bytes, asset, adversary, key and nonce ownership, tamper rejection, secret-safe failure, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result cryptographically secure. This does not satisfy Crypto Go Forward Secrecy Ephemeral for authenticated X25519 key-agreement transcript.
The record names the assumption, applies Crypto Go Forward Secrecy Ephemeral, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Crypto Go Forward Secrecy Ephemeral record says authenticated X25519 key-agreement transcript looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Crypto Go Forward Secrecy Ephemeral.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Rotate and erase ephemeral private keys, prevent reuse, bind roles, and state when compromise does or does not expose prior sessions.
